steps.title: Identify the function of the highlighted sentence
steps.content: The sentence begins with "However," signaling a pivot away from Lai and colleagues' findings. What follows raises a concern about the study: it questions whether the methodology (or conclusion) is sound. The sentence's purpose is therefore to flag a potential flaw in a scientific study, not to report a finding or introduce a debate.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the match
steps.content:
• Choice A — To note a potential concern about the findings of a scientific study: Matches the sentence's function — it uses "However" to introduce skepticism about Lai et al.'s study. ✓
  • Choice B — To explain how the behavior of a fish species has changed overtime: Misreads the sentence as describing behavioral change in the species itself rather than questioning the study's methodology. ✗
  • Choice C — To discuss an aspect of ocean acidifcation that is frequently overlooked: Confuses ocean acidification as a subject of analysis rather than a variable in the study's execution. ✗
  • Choice D — To present a debate between two research teams about a cause of ocean acidification: No such debate between teams appears in the passage. ✗
That is the correct answer.
